core_mechanism: singular perturbation / multiple-scale asymptotic limit in which coupled small parameters tend to zero at a fixed ratio, yielding uniform a priori bounds and convergence of full solutions (from well-prepared initial data) to those of a reduced limit system
brief_reason: The abstract establishes uniform boundedness as two small parameters (Froude/Rossby numbers) tend to zero with fixed ratio and proves convergence of well-prepared solutions to limit equations, including a three-scale limit where one parameter vanishes faster — the hallmark of a multi-scale singular-limit reduction.
